General Use.principal_or_secondary: May be used as principal or secondary diagnosis, depending on encounter context.
General Use.first_listed_only: Some Z codes may be reported only as principal/first-listed.
General Use.procedure_codes: Z codes are not procedure codes—must accompany procedure code if procedure performed.

Contact_or_Exposure.usage: May be first-listed (e.g., for testing) or secondary (e.g., risk factor).

Inoculations_and_Vaccinations.note: Requires procedure codes for administration/type; may be secondary if routine.

Status_Codes.purpose: Indicate carrier state, residual effects, or presence of devices.
Status_Codes.distinct_from_history: History code = condition no longer exists; status = ongoing state.
Status_Codes.avoid_redundancy: Do not code if included in diagnosis code.

examples.Z68: BMI (with associated diagnosis; not in pregnancy)
examples.Z79: Long-term drug therapy (not for addiction/short-term use)
examples.Z92.82: Status post tPA within last 24h (receiving facility only)

History_Codes.personal: Past condition, no treatment, potential for recurrence.
History_Codes.use: May accompany follow-up/screening codes; sequenced after reason for encounter.

categories.Z83: Family history of other specific disorders
categories.Z91.4–Z91.85: Personal history of trauma, self-harm, falling, military service
categories.Z92: Personal history of medical treatment (except contraception, tPA)

Screening.definition: Testing well individuals for early disease detection.
Screening.rules: First-listed if screening is main reason; additional if done with other visits; not used for diagnostic exams.

categories.Z13: Screening for other diseases/disorders (except Z13.9 unspecified)

Observation.purpose: Used when suspected condition ruled out; limited use.
Observation.rules: Not for confirmed conditions or if symptoms/injury present.

categories.Z03: Observation for suspected disease ruled out
categories.Z04: Examination/observation for other reasons (except Z04.9 unspecified)
categories.Z05: Observation of newborn for suspected condition ruled out

Aftercare.definition: Continued care after initial treatment completed.
Aftercare.exclusions: Not for active treatment or injury aftercare.

Follow_up.definition: Surveillance after completed treatment; condition no longer exists.
Follow_up.sequence: Follow-up code first, history code second.

Donor.Z52: Donors of organs/tissues (living only; not cadaveric)

Counseling.definition: Assistance after illness/injury or for social/family issues.

Obstetrical_and_Reproductive.note: Used when no OB complications present.

Routine_and_Administrative_Exams.abnormal_findings: Code based on findings known at coding time.

Social_Determinants_of_Health.sources: May be based on provider or non-provider documentation (if signed-off).
